title: Dolianthus subalpinus
taxonomy: Dolianthus subalpinus belongs to the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the coffee or madder family. It is a genus of flowering plants native to China. The specific classification of Dolianthus subalpinus is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Asterids, Order: Gentianales, Family: Rubiaceae, Genus: Dolianthus, Species: D. subalpinus. The classification shows that Dolianthus subalpinus is a part of the larger group of flowering plants. The specific classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships of Dolianthus subalpinus with other plants.
uses: Dolianthus subalpinus is a plant species known to have various medicinal uses. Its extracts have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues and coughs due to its expectorant properties. In addition, it is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The plant is also thought to have antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which may aid in the treatment of infections and skin conditions. Further research is needed to fully explore the medicinal potential of Dolianthus subalpinus.
